skill.name: directory-submissions
skill.description: When the user wants to submit their product to startup, SaaS, AI, agent, MCP, no-code, or review directories for backlinks, domain rating, and discovery. Also use when the user mentions "directory submissions," "submit to directories," "backlinks from directories," "list my product," "submit to Product Hunt," "BetaList," "TAAFT," "Futurepedia," "G2 listing," "Capterra listing," "AlternativeTo," "SaaSHub," "AI directories," "MCP registry," "agent directory," "dofollow backlinks," "launch directories," or "directory tracker." Use this whenever someone is planning the directory layer of a product launch or an ongoing backlink campaign. For the broader launch moment, see launch. For programmatic SEO pages that should live behind these backlinks, see programmatic-seo. For AI citation optimization, see ai-seo.
skill.summary: Plan and execute directory submissions to high-DR startup, SaaS, AI, MCP, and review directories with tiered positioning variants and a Product Hunt 3-week prep timeline.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: The skill is content-only (markdown guidance plus a CSV directory list) and contains no executable code, no real network calls, no filesystem writes, and no credential access. All 421 static findings are false positives: markdown backticks triggered 'shell execution' detectors, plain English words like 'at' and 'system' triggered 'command scheduling' and 'reconnaissance' rules, and the CSV's directory URLs triggered 'hardcoded URL' alerts even though they are the intended data. The CRITICAL heuristic alert (code+network+credentials) does not apply because none of those primitives are present. Safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False positive: Ruby/shell backtick execution markers
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown formatting backticks (e.g. `[product]`, `https://directory.com/...`) as backtick execution. All matches are inline code spans inside prose, not Ruby or shell.

low_findings.title: False positive: Hardcoded URL pattern
low_findings.description: URLs appear in the directory catalog and the submission tracker CSV as intended data (submission targets and listings). They are not exfiltration endpoints or runtime network calls.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Pattern matched substrings like 'MD' (markdown), 'DES' (designed/description), 'SHA' (shape), and similar in plain English marketing text. No cryptographic code or algorithm references exist in the skill.

low_findings.title: False positive: Pastebin flagged as data exfiltration
low_findings.description: submission-tracker-template.csv:149 lists Pastebin as a Tier 8 profile/content platform directory. The skill recommends users create a Pastebin profile with a backlink to their product, which is a legitimate directory-submission tactic. This is not data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False positive: 'SAM' substring matched Windows SAM database
low_findings.description: positioning-variations.md mentions 'SAML' (the SSO standard) in the technical tag library. The 'SAM' substring is a false match for the Windows SAM database pattern.

low_findings.title: False positive: 'at command scheduling' and 'system reconnaissance'
low_findings.description: Pattern matched natural English words 'at' (preposition) and 'system' (used in business/systematic contexts) rather than the cron `at` utility or system probe commands. No scheduling or reconnaissance code exists.

content.user_title: Submit Your Product to High-DR Directories
content.value_statement: Manual directory submission is a tedious copy-paste process that demands per-tier positioning variants. This skill gives a tiered directory catalog, per-tier positioning copy, a tracker template, and the Product Hunt 3-week prep timeline.
actual_capabilities: Run a 9-question Phase 0 readiness assessment before recommending any submission
Recommend a tier mix from a 200+ directory catalog (Tiers 1-13) tailored to product type
Generate per-tier positioning variants (tagline, 60-char short, 150-word long) from the variant library
Provide the 3-week Product Hunt prep timeline mapped to calendar dates
Apply the G2 10-in-30 reviews protocol with cutoff dates and badge mechanics
Track submissions in the provided CSV template with dofollow verification steps
limitations: Does not submit to directories automatically; the user must complete each form manually
Domain Rating values are approximate and drift; re-verify via Ahrefs before planning around a number
Does not generate destination pages (alternatives, use cases, templates); it directs you to build them first
Positioning variants are templates with placeholders; you supply the actual product details

use_cases.title: Plan a directory launch for a new SaaS
use_cases.description: Run Phase 0 readiness, pick the right tier mix, sequence submissions across 3 weeks, and prepare per-tier positioning variants for the SaaS directory landscape.
use_cases.target_user: SaaS founder preparing for launch week

use_cases.title: Build an ongoing backlink campaign
use_cases.description: Maintain rolling submissions to Tier 2-8 directories after launch, verify dofollow status, and integrate with ongoing content, reviews, and community distribution.
use_cases.target_user: Growth marketer running an always-on SEO program

use_cases.title: List an MCP server on agent registries
use_cases.description: Submit to Glama, APITracker, Linux Foundation MCP Registry, and AI Agents List with MCP-native positioning variants and an A-grade security posture.
use_cases.target_user: AI agent or MCP server developer

prompt_templates.title: Quick directory plan for a new product
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me submit my new product to the right directories. Run the Phase 0 readiness check, then give me a tier mix and a 3-week submission order.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Product Hunt timeline to a launch date
prompt_templates.prompt: We are launching on [date]. Map the 3-week Product Hunt prep timeline to specific calendar dates and list what I need to do each day.
prompt_templates.scenario: Founder with a fixed launch date

prompt_templates.title: Tier mix for an AI-native SaaS with MCP
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a tier mix for an AI-native SaaS that has both MCP server capabilities and no-code audiences. Show which directories to prioritize and which to skip.

prompt_templates.title: Audit existing submissions and plan a Tier-2 push
prompt_templates.prompt: We already submitted to 30 directories in Tier 1. Audit which Tier 2 directories are the highest-leverage next 20 to add this month, and flag any Tier 1 listings that need refreshing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Post-launch growth marketer optimizing the program

output_examples.input: We are launching our AI SaaS in 3 weeks. Help me plan.
output_examples.output: Phase 0 status: 6 of 9 ready. Hard blocks: no pricing page, no demo video. Soft blocks: only 2 alt pages live, no template gallery. Tier mix: T1 flagship (15 directories, Product Hunt as anchor on Tue Apr 9 at 12:01 AM PT), T2 SaaS (50), T3 AI (40), T4 MCP (10). 3-week PH prep timeline mapped below. Reviews target: run the 10-in-30 protocol in launch month for G2 Grid appearance. Day-30 KPI: 30 referring domains, 20 DR. Day-90: 80 referring domains, 30+ DR.

output_examples.input: Can I just copy-paste the same description into every directory?
output_examples.output: No. Rule 3: positioning varies by directory type, and AI engines (Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity) penalize duplicate content. Use four distinct variants: startup directories lead with outcome (audience is founders), SaaS directories lead with alternative framing (people search [competitor] alternative), AI directories lead with AI-first architecture, and B2B review sites lead with ROI and use case. The full variant library is in references/positioning-variations.md.

best_practices: Build destination pages (alternatives, use cases, templates) before submitting to any directory so the link equity has somewhere useful to land
Vary the opening sentence of every per-tier description to avoid AI duplicate-content penalties
Run the 10-in-30 reviews protocol in launch month to hit the G2 Grid threshold of 10 reviews
anti_patterns: Paying for $60 to $200 mass-submission services; they target DR-under-10 spam directories that dilute your backlink profile
Submitting to directories before the destination page is live, indexed, and GEO-ready
Asking for upvotes on Product Hunt; the 2026 algorithm penalizes upvote-begging and rewards feedback requests

faq.question: Does this skill submit to directories automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides the manual copy-paste submission process and tracks it in a CSV template.

faq.question: Why are Domain Rating values marked as approximate?
faq.answer: DR shifts monthly as backlink profiles change. Re-verify via Ahrefs or Moz before planning around any specific number.

faq.question: Can I copy-paste the same description into every directory?
faq.answer: No. AI engines penalize duplicate content. Use a per-tier variant from the positioning library instead.

faq.question: How many reviews do I need on G2 for the Grid?
faq.answer: 10 reviews is the magic threshold for Grid appearance. Use the 10-in-30 protocol in launch month to hit it.

faq.question: When should I launch on Product Hunt?
faq.answer: Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday at 12:01 AM Pacific Time. Weekend launches get 60 to 70 percent less traffic.

faq.question: Should I pay for the G2 paid plan in year one?
faq.answer: No. The free listing plus the Users Love Us badge (20 reviews at 4.0 or higher) is sufficient until you reach scale.
